---
title: "UX_R001: Single-Click Navigation"
subtitle: "All drill-down, return, and navigation actions use single-click — never double-click"
category: "Rule"
type: "Rule"
id: "UX_R001"
created: "2026-03-14"
triggered_by: "Issue #351 — state map return originally required double-click"
---

## Summary

All user navigation actions — including drill-down into detail views and returning to parent views — **must** use single-click. Double-click is never required for navigation in this application.

## Motivation

Double-click is unintuitive for navigation:

1. **Discoverability**: Users don't expect to double-click on a data visualization — there's no visual affordance
2. **Consistency**: All other interactions in the dashboard (tab switching, filter selection, button clicks) use single-click
3. **Accessibility**: Double-click is harder for users with motor impairments
4. **Touch devices**: Double-tap on mobile/tablet is unreliable and often triggers zoom instead

## Rule

```
RULE UX_R001_SINGLE_CLICK_NAV:
FOR ALL navigation actions A in the dashboard:
  A MUST be triggered by single-click
  A MUST NOT require double-click
APPLIES TO:
  - Drill-down (e.g., world map -> country states)
  - Return to parent (e.g., states -> world map)
  - Tab switching
  - Filter selection
  - Any action that changes the current view
```

## Application

| Action | Correct | Wrong |
|--------|---------|-------|
| Drill into US states | Single-click on US | Double-click on US |
| Return to world map | Single-click on state map | Double-click on state map |
| Switch tabs | Single-click on tab | — |
| Select filter value | Single-click on dropdown | — |
